## reference · concise scannable web writing

* Web users **scan** text, they don't read it thoroughly.
* Users prefer **short, concise** text, not long scrolling pages.
* Users dislike **marketing fluff** and "marketese"; they prefer **factual information**.
* Effective web writing styles:
  * **Concise**: improves usability by 58%.
  * **Scannable**: improves usability by 47%.
  * **Objective**: improves usability by 27%.
* Combining concise, scannable, and objective writing styles results in **124% higher measured usability**.
* Key guidelines for good web writing:
  * Carefully **organize information**.
  * Use **topic sentences**.
  * Limit each **paragraph to one main idea**.
  * Provide the **right amount of information**.
* Users appreciate **summaries** and the **inverted pyramid writing style**.
* **Hypertext structure** can be helpful if done correctly.

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/concise-scannable-and-objective-how-to-write-for-the-web/
